disclaimer: 本檔由 PDF 自動萃取，levels 為 ACGME 英文原文；name_zh 由內建翻譯表提供，未匹配項目以英文呈現，可後續人工校對補上中譯。

levels.1: Acquires a foundational history for common infectious diseases and syndromes Performs a foundational physical examination
levels.2: Acquires a complete history, including specific host and environmental factors Performs an examination that elicits common or straightforward infectious diseases and syndromes
levels.3: Acquires a detailed history incorporating pertinent supplemental information Performs an examination that elicits uncommon or complicated infectious diseases and syndromes
levels.4: Acquires a tailored history that incorporates epidemiology, past clinical data, and nuances specific for suspected pathogens or syndromes Performs a tailored examination that elicits subtle findings of infectious diseases and syndromes
levels.5: Serves as a role model in obtaining a history that identifies subtle details and resolves ambiguity in the patient history Serves as a role model who has mastered the art of examination that helps in making a definitive diagnosis

subcompetencies.name_en: Management of Patients with Possible and Proven Infectious Diseases
subcompetencies.name_zh: Management of Patients with Possible and Proven Infectious Diseases

levels.1: Develops an initial assessment for patients with low-complexity conditions
levels.2: Develops initial and follow-up management plans for patients with low-complexity conditions
levels.3: Develops an initial and follow-up plan for patients with moderate-complexity conditions and adjusts the plan over the course of clinical care
levels.4: Develops a comprehensive management plan, including contingency plans for patients with complex conditions
levels.5: Develops customized management plans for all patients, regardless of the complexity of the condition, and incorporating diagnostic uncertainty and cost effectiveness

levels.1: Respectfully responds to a consultation request and conveys recommendations, with supervision Recognizes consult acuity and urgency, with supervision
levels.2: Identifies and clarifies the clinical questions and recommendations for the consultation Recognizes consult acuity and urgency independently
levels.3: Seeks and integrates input from different members of the health care team and provides recommendations to the primary team in a clear and timely manner Prioritizes workflow in response to consult acuity and urgency
levels.4: Provides comprehensive and prioritized recommendations, including assessment and rationale to all necessary health care team members Mobilizes resources to provide care in an urgent situation
levels.5: Leads the health care team in the provision of effective consultative services across the spectrum of disease complexity and acuity

levels.1: Integrates patient-specific information to generate a limited differential diagnosis
levels.2: Provides a limited prioritized differential diagnosis using supporting rationale
levels.3: Formulates a prioritized differential diagnosis; demonstrates the ability to modify a diagnosis based on a patient’s clinical course and additional data
levels.4: Formulates a differential diagnosis to include atypical presentations and uncommon disorders; recognizes sources of diagnostic error
levels.5: Formulates a differential diagnosis to include newly recognized and emerging conditions

levels.1: Demonstrates foundational knowledge of diagnostic evaluation for pathogens Demonstrates foundational knowledge of diagnostic evaluation for clinical syndromes
levels.2: Applies knowledge of diagnostic evaluation for common pathogens Applies knowledge of indications for diagnostic evaluation for common clinical syndromes
levels.3: Applies knowledge of indications for diagnostic evaluation of uncommon pathogens, antimicrobial resistance, and therapeutic drug monitoring Applies diagnostic testing in consideration of risks, benefits, and consequences for clinical syndromes
levels.4: Interprets diagnostic evaluations for pathogens and clinical syndromes, considering performance characteristics, limitations, and nuances
levels.5: Teaches others the nuances of directed diagnostic evaluation for pathogens and clinical syndromes

subcompetencies.name_en: Treatment and Therapeutics including Anti-Infectives, Immunoprophylaxis, and Adjunctive Therapies
subcompetencies.name_zh: Treatment and Therapeutics including Anti-Infectives, Immunoprophylaxis, and Adjunctive Therapies

levels.1: Demonstrates foundational knowledge of common anti-infectives, including spectrum of activity, contraindications, and clinical indications
levels.2: Demonstrates knowledge of common anti-infectives, immunoprophylaxis, and adjunctive therapies, including dosing, monitoring, and adverse effects
levels.3: Demonstrates knowledge of common anti-infectives, immunoprophylaxis, and adjunctive therapies, including resistance mechanisms, drug interactions, and relative effectiveness
levels.4: Demonstrates knowledge of uncommon anti-infectives, immunoprophylaxis, and adjunctive therapies, including dosing, monitoring, resistance mechanisms, drug interactions, adverse effects, and relative effectiveness
levels.5: Teaches others the nuances of anti-infectives, immunoprophylaxis, and adjunctive therapies

subcompetencies.name_en: Infection Control/Prevention, Antimicrobial Stewardship, and Epidemiology
subcompetencies.name_zh: Infection Control/Prevention, Antimicrobial Stewardship, and Epidemiology

levels.1: Demonstrates foundational knowledge of the principles of infection prevention Demonstrates foundational knowledge of the principles of antimicrobial stewardship
levels.2: Implements infection prevention measures for common situations Implements antimicrobial stewardship practices for common situations Demonstrates basic knowledge of the principles of epidemiology
levels.3: Implements infection prevention measures for uncommon situations Implements antimicrobial stewardship practices for uncommon situations Practices the basic principles of epidemiology, including identifying and responding to common epidemiological events
levels.4: Teaches infection prevention practices to health care providers, patients, and the medical community Teaches health care providers, patients, and the medical community antimicrobial stewardship practices Teaches on the epidemiological impact of infectious diseases on population health
levels.5: Demonstrates innovation and leadership in infection prevention practices Demonstrates innovation and leadership in antimicrobial stewardship practices Leads a team in identifying and responding to epidemiological events

levels.1: Demonstrates basic knowledge of pathophysiological and foundational science pertaining to common infectious diseases and host response
levels.2: Demonstrates advanced knowledge of pathophysiological and foundational science concepts pertaining to common infectious diseases and host response
levels.3: Applies advanced knowledge of pathophysiological and foundational science concepts pertaining to common infectious diseases and host response
levels.4: Applies advanced knowledge of pathophysiological and foundational science concepts pertaining to uncommon infectious diseases and host response
levels.5: Applies comprehensive knowledge of pathophysiology and foundational science pertaining to new and emerging infectious diseases and issues of host response

levels.1: Identifies areas worthy of scholarly activity
levels.2: Plans a scholarly activity
levels.3: Implements scholarly activity
levels.4: Disseminates independent scholarly work, locally or regionally, that has generated new medical knowledge, educational programs, or process improvement
levels.5: Disseminates independent scholarly work, nationally or internationally, that has generated new medical knowledge, educational programs, or process improvement

levels.1: Identifies patient safety events and discloses them to leadership, patients, and/or patients’ family members Demonstrates knowledge of how to report patient safety events
levels.2: Analyzes the factors that contribute to patient safety events Reports patient safety events using the health system’s reporting mechanism
levels.3: Offers prevention strategies to mitigate patient safety events
levels.4: Participates in efforts to modify systems to prevent patient safety events
levels.5: Leads efforts to modify systems to prevent patient safety events

levels.1: Demonstrates basic knowledge of quality improvement methodologies and metrics
levels.2: Identifies opportunities for quality improvement projects
levels.3: Participates in quality improvement projects
levels.4: Demonstrates the skills required to identify, develop, implement, and analyze a quality improvement project
levels.5: Leads quality improvement projects

levels.1: Identifies key elements for safe and effective transitions and coordination of care and hand-offs
levels.2: Performs safe and effective transitions and coordination of care and hand-offs in routine clinical situations
levels.3: Performs safe and effective transitions and coordination of care/hand-offs in complex clinical situations
levels.4: Role models and advocates for safe and effective transitions and coordination of care/hand-offs within and across health care delivery systems
levels.5: Improves quality of transitions and coordination of care within and across health care delivery systems to optimize patient outcomes

levels.1: Demonstrates knowledge of population and community health needs
levels.2: Identifies specific population and community health needs for the local population
levels.3: Identifies local resources effectively to meet the needs of a patient population and community
levels.4: Uses local resources effectively to meet the needs of a patient population and community
levels.5: Leads innovations and advocates for populations and communities with health care needs

subcompetencies.name_en: Physician Role in Health Care Systems
subcompetencies.name_zh: 醫師於醫療體系中的角色

levels.1: Identifies key components of the health care system Describes basic health payment systems
levels.2: Describes how components of a complex health care system are interrelated, and how this impacts patient care Delivers care with consideration of each patient’s payment model
levels.3: Discusses how individual practice affects the broader system Engages with patients in shared decision making, informed by each patient’s payment models
levels.4: Manages various components of the complex health care system to provide efficient and effective patient care Advocates for patient care needs with consideration of the limitations of each patient’s payment model
levels.5: Advocates for or leads systems change that enhances high-value, efficient, and effective patient care Actively engaged in influencing health policy through advocacy activities at the local, regional, or national level

levels.1: Demonstrates how to access and use available evidence for patient care and elicits patient preferences and values to guide care
levels.2: Locates and applies the best available evidence, integrated with patient values and preferences, to the care of patients with common conditions
levels.3: Locates and applies the best available evidence, integrated with patient values and preference, to the care of patients with complex conditions
levels.4: Critically appraises conflicting or ambiguous evidence to guide individualized patient care
levels.5: Mentors others to critically appraise and apply evidence

subcompetencies.name_en: Reflective Practice and Commitment to Personal Growth
subcompetencies.name_zh: 反思實踐與個人成長承諾

levels.1: Identifies personal and professional development goals and recognizes gaps between expectations and actual performance Actively seeks opportunities to improve
levels.2: Demonstrates openness to performance data (feedback and other input), and analyzes and reflects on gaps to inform goals Designs and implements a learning plan, with prompting
levels.3: Seeks performance data episodically, with adaptability and humility, and institutes behavioral changes to narrow the gaps between expectations and actual performance Independently creates and implements a learning plan
levels.4: Intentionally seeks performance data consistently with adaptability and humility, considering alternatives in narrowing the gaps between expectations and actual performance Uses performance data to measure the effectiveness of the learning plan and improves it when necessary
levels.5: Role models commitment to self-improvement and coaches others on reflective practice Facilitates the design and implementation of learning plans for others

levels.1: Demonstrates professional behavior in routine situations
levels.2: Identifies and describes potential personal triggers for professionalism lapses and takes responsibility for one’s own professionalism lapses
levels.3: Demonstrates a pattern of professional behavior in complex or stressful situations; describes when and where to report professionalism lapses
levels.4: Recognizes situations that may trigger professionalism lapses and intervenes to prevent lapses in oneself and others
levels.5: Coaches others when their behavior fails to meet professional expectations

levels.1: Demonstrates knowledge of ethical principles
levels.2: Applies ethical principles to address straightforward situations
levels.3: Analyzes complex situations using ethical principles and identifies the need to seek help in addressing complex situations
levels.4: Uses appropriate resources for managing and resolving ethical dilemmas as needed
levels.5: Identifies and seeks to address system-level factors that induce or exacerbate ethical problems or impede their resolution

levels.1: Performs tasks and responsibilities with prompting
levels.2: Recognizes factors that may impact one’s own ability to complete tasks and responsibilities in a timely manner and describes strategies for improvement
levels.3: Performs tasks and responsibilities in a timely manner with appropriate attention to detail in routine situations
levels.4: Performs tasks and responsibilities in a timely manner with appropriate attention to detail in complex or stressful situations
levels.5: Creates strategies to enhance others ability to complete administrative tasks and patient care efficiently

levels.1: Recognizes the importance of addressing personal and professional well-being
levels.2: Lists available resources for personal and professional well-being Describes institutional resources that are meant to promote well-being
levels.3: With assistance, proposes a plan to promote personal and professional well-being Recognizes which institutional factors affect well-being
levels.4: Independently develops a plan to promote personal and professional well-being Describes institutional factors that positively and/or negatively affect well-being
levels.5: Creates institutional level interventions that promote colleagues’ well-being Describes institutional programs designed to examine systemic contributors to burnout

subcompetencies.name_zh: 以病人與家屬為中心之溝通

levels.1: Uses clear language and non-verbal behavior to demonstrate respect and establish rapport Identifies common barriers to effective communication
levels.2: Establishes and maintains a therapeutic relationship with the patient using active listening and clear language in straightforward encounters Identifies complex barriers to effective communication
levels.3: Establishes and maintains a therapeutic relationship using effective communication behaviors in challenging patient encounters Adjusts communication strategies based on identified barriers, incorporating patient and caregiver expectations and goals of care
levels.4: Establishes and maintains therapeutic relationships using shared decision making, regardless of complexity Proactively improves communication by addressing barriers and biases
levels.5: Serves as a role model in establishing respectful, culturally sensitive therapeutic relationships while mitigating communication barriers and engaging in critical self-reflection

levels.1: Communicates information effectively in a manner that demonstrates respect for all members of the interprofessional team
levels.2: Solicits feedback on performance as a member of the health care team and adjusts communication approach to team needs, promoting open and safe communication
levels.3: Facilitates interprofessional team communication to reconcile conflict and provides constructive feedback to team members
levels.4: Leads and facilitates regular interdisciplinary discussions, including in complex and challenging situations
levels.5: Models and coaches flexible communication strategies that facilitate excellence in interprofessional teamwork

levels.1: Accurately documents comprehensive and current information Communicates using formats specified by institutional policy to safeguard patient personal health information
levels.2: Documents encounter, including clinical reasoning in an organized manner Selects direct and indirect forms of communication based on context, with guidance
levels.3: Documents encounter through prioritized and concise yet thorough notes Independently selects direct and indirect forms of communication based on context
levels.4: Concisely documents clinical reasoning, including anticipatory guidance, while satisfying institutional billing needs and compliance Models effective written and verbal communication
levels.5: Coaches others in accurately documenting diagnostic and therapeutic reasoning Guides departmental or institutional communication policies and procedures
